description: Fix data-loss window: don't mark corrections synthesized until rules are confirmed created

tasks.title: Split export_and_mark_synthesized into export-only + mark-only
tasks.description: In corrections.rs, refactor export_and_mark_synthesized into two functions: (1) export_corrections_only — builds export JSON and writes file, returns the exported highlight_ids, does NOT update synthesized_at. (2) mark_corrections_synthesized — takes highlight_ids, sets synthesized_at = now(). The existing export_corrections_json Tauri command calls export-only. Add a new mark_corrections_synthesized Tauri command. Keep mark_unsynthesized as recovery path unchanged.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `cargo check --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml` - exits with code 0
File `src-tauri/src/commands/corrections.rs` contains a function `export_corrections_only` that does NOT call UPDATE synthesized_at
File `src-tauri/src/commands/corrections.rs` contains a Tauri command `mark_corrections_synthesized` that takes highlight_ids

tasks.title: Add margin_mark_corrections_synthesized MCP tool
tasks.description: Add a new MCP tool that marks corrections as synthesized. This is what Claude calls AFTER successfully creating rules from the exported corrections. The tool takes an array of highlight_ids and sets synthesized_at = now() on each. This closes the transaction gap: corrections stay unsynthesized until the agent confirms rule creation succeeded.
acceptanceCriteria: File `mcp/src/tools/corrections.ts` or equivalent contains a `margin_mark_corrections_synthesized` handler
The tool accepts `highlight_ids: string[]` and updates `synthesized_at` in the database
Run `cd mcp && npm run build` - exits with code 0

tasks.title: Update frontend to not mark on export
tasks.description: Update exportCorrectionsJson in tauri-commands.ts to match the new return type (highlight_ids + count instead of just count). Update StyleMemorySection.tsx handleExportForSynthesis to include the highlight_ids in the copied synthesis prompt so Claude can call margin_mark_corrections_synthesized after creating rules. Update the synthesis prompt template to instruct Claude to call the mark tool after all rules are created.
acceptanceCriteria: File `src/lib/tauri-commands.ts` - exportCorrectionsJson returns an object with highlight_ids
File `src/components/settings/StyleMemorySection.tsx` includes `margin_mark_corrections_synthesized` in the clipboard prompt

tasks.title: Write failing test for partial synthesis data loss
tasks.description: Add a Rust test that reproduces the data-loss scenario: export corrections (should NOT mark synthesized), verify corrections are still unsynthesized, then mark synthesized explicitly. Also test: if mark_synthesized is never called, a second export returns the same corrections (not lost). This is the regression test that proves the fix works.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml` - exits with code 0
File `src-tauri/src/commands/corrections.rs` contains a test named `export_without_mark_keeps_corrections_unsynthesized` or similar
File `src-tauri/src/commands/corrections.rs` contains a test that verifies re-export returns same items when mark was never called

tasks.title: Write MCP integration test for mark_corrections_synthesized
tasks.description: Add an MCP test that exercises the full flow: create correction → export → create rule → mark synthesized → verify correction is no longer in unsynthesized list. Also test: export → don't mark → corrections still appear as unsynthesized.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `cd mcp && npm test` - exits with code 0
A test file contains a test for `margin_mark_corrections_synthesized` tool

tasks.title: Update existing tests to match new export behavior
tasks.description: The existing tests (export_and_mark_synthesized_preserves_rows_with_timestamp, export_second_time_returns_zero_items, export_and_mark_preserves_backfilled_rows) assume export marks synthesized_at. Update them: export should NOT mark synthesized, a separate mark call should. Keep test intent but adapt to the split API.
